title: Three Days Significance
wikitext:
[]: Three Days Significance?             Quote Post by AsukaChan » Sun Feb 17, 2008 7:00 am I questioned if this was Discussion worthy, but feel free to move me if I'm wrong (as I always say, to cover my ass XD). Maybe I'm just over-tired and a little out of it with being sick, but it occurred to me that there's a repetition of the use of something being three days apart from something else. I remember it happening twice, but blanked out on it before I noticed anything else (or did the math). That could've just been a reasonable amount of time passing for the writers to explain why Toji and Kensuke, for example, would start worrying about Shinji, but then I wondered if it could have been another little intentional thing and thought it was worth mentioning, even if it really isn't that important. I dunno, I just noticed that on that third day everything seems to happen. XD

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Feb 17, 2008 7:08 am "And on the third day..." Standard mythic trope.

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Sun Feb 17, 2008 5:38 pm The sun rises in the same place along the horizon for 3 days at each equinox (assuming you're at the right latitude) before swinging back on itself, the days after will increase or decrease in length and brightness - when used in a story it's saying that something has made a 180, where once there was darkness there is now light, or where light once was there is now darkness, what was dead is now reborn, or what was alive is now dead - for the winter and spring equinoxes respectively. There is a lot to be said about the number 3 in general, but I think that's where the 3 days thing comes from.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Sun Feb 17, 2008 7:43 pm "On the third day of Christmas my true love sent to me/ Three calling birds, two french hens and a partridge in a pear tree" Or perhaps the battle of Thermopylae, over in three days according to the Father of Lies (Herodotus)? Like Tines said, three is a recurring number in myths and legends worldwide, and it's remains a trope, especially in present-day literature. Just look at the number of literary triologies people put out.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Feb 17, 2008 7:53 pm Every number is "significant" if you look hard enough. They've devoted a whole -ology to this sort of thing.

[]:             Quote Post by AsukaChan » Mon Feb 18, 2008 2:08 am XD I thought as much. I even came across a "Significance of the Number Three" site like two seconds before I posted it. I was wondering more if the events themselves had any significance, but then I guess that's related, again, to the use of the number three and simple plot devices.

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Tue Feb 19, 2008 10:22 am Reichu wrote: Every number is "significant" if you look hard enough. They've devoted a whole -ology to this sort of thing.

[]:             Quote Post by evamoth » Fri Feb 22, 2008 5:52 pm also Jesus rose again 3 days after dieing on the cross

[]:             Quote Post by Rommel » Mon Feb 25, 2008 8:45 pm I'm surprised it took this many posts for someone to state the blatantly obvious.

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Tue Feb 26, 2008 5:47 am Rommel wrote: I'm surprised it took this many posts for someone to state the blatantly obvious.
